title: Grader (on-disk form)
description: Schema for one grader YAML file under .tessary/graders/. The authoritative validator is validate.py (at the plugin root), which enforces these structural rules plus cross-field invariants and optional pipeline.yaml / bundle cross-references. This schema is the contract any grader author must satisfy; validate.py is the enforcer. v2 added: self_tests[].category (self_tests removed in v7 — see below), applies_when_check (deterministic graders; removed in v9 — see below), per-grader runtime fields (block_on_fail, dataset_refs, and the v9-removed owner / cost / latency budgets), and a _meta provenance block for survivable re-runs. v3 adds kind=score (continuous 1-5 quality scoring). v4 adds scope=trace (multi-turn: grade the final turn given prior n-1 messages) and kind=agentic (binary verdict produced by an agent running in a sandbox via agent_spec; emitted here, executed by the runner). v5 adds no grader fields (the change is the call-site default_grade_mode flag plus pinned trace history sourcing). v6: applies_when is ALWAYS evaluated by an LLM at runtime — for kind=deterministic the platform runs a separate LLM applicability gate before the (gate-free) deterministic_check; for llm_judge/score it rides inline in the judge prompt. applies_when_check is REMOVED (the gate is never compiled). Trace deterministic checks receive structured input (messages + tool_uses). v7 REMOVES per-grader self_tests entirely: graders no longer carry hand-authored self-test cases, the self_test_pass_rate/self_test_variance calibration fields, or the applies_when ↔ not_applicable self-test invariant. A grader's behavior is now validated platform-side against reusable golden datasets — real spans associated with the grader and labeled per (grader, dataset_item) — so the author emits only the verdict body, applies_when, confidence, and rationale. v8 DEFERS judge-prompt authoring to the platform for kind=llm_judge and kind=score: the author emits the definition (kind, applies_when, rubric_levels+score_scale for score, confidence, rationale) plus a top-level marker `_body_source: platform` and does NOT author judge_prompt/rubric; the platform expands the verdict body on import. When `_body_source` is absent the v7 requirements still hold (so deterministic/execution/agentic bodies and any legacy llm_judge/score files keep validating). v8 also adds the orchestrator-owned call-site field expected_spans (telemetry nomenclature extracted from the call site's code) — schema'd on the call_site shard, not on the grader. v9 makes three changes: (1) REMOVES the redundant grader fields applies_when_check (already removed as a feature in v6), owner, cost_budget_tokens, and latency_budget_ms_p95, and the grader-level compliance_tags (the FAILURE-MODE-level compliance_tags is unchanged and still consumed); all were unread or write-only on the grader. (2) Adds the body LIFECYCLE: the `_body_source` enum is widened to {platform, platform-materialized, human} so a platform-MATERIALIZED body (synced back into the repo by the platform's GitHub integration) and a HUMAN-edited body are first-class on-disk states — for these two values the inline judge_prompt/rubric MUST be PRESENT (the inverse of `platform`, which requires it empty); _meta gains optional materialized_at + body_digest (a canonical hash used to detect a human edit of a materialized body). (3) expected_spans entries gain `source: observed | inferred` (default inferred) on the call_site shard so OTel-observed span names are trusted unconditionally and `confidence` ranks only inferred guesses. All v9 changes are additive/optional-defaulted: every existing v8 bundle still validates. The $id tracks the current contract version (9).

id.description: Stable grader ID. For single_call and trace: `<failure_mode_id>::grader`. For chain: `<chain_id>::<failure_name>::grader`. Attached by orchestrator, not author.

scope.description: single_call: grades one call's output. chain: grades a relationship across N distinct call-site outputs in a session. trace: grades the final turn of a multi-turn session given the prior n-1 messages as context — anchored to one call_site_id like single_call.

failure_mode_id.description: Required for failure-catching kinds (llm_judge, deterministic, execution). The failure mode this grader catches.

quality_dimension_id.description: Required for kind=score. The quality dimension this grader scores (parallel to failure_mode_id). Resolves to a pipeline quality_dimensions[].id; validate.py --bundle enforces the bijection.

call_site_id.description: Required and non-empty when scope=single_call or scope=trace; must be null/absent when scope=chain.

chain_id.description: Required and non-empty when scope=chain; must be null/absent when scope=single_call.

taxonomy_node_id.description: Attached by orchestrator at the taxonomy step, before grader synthesis fan-out, so subagents can splice it onto each grader at write time. Required for failure-catching kinds; absent for kind=score (quality dimensions are not taxonomy-clustered).

agent_spec.description: Required for kind=agentic. Declares an agent that produces the verdict by multi-turn analysis in a sandbox (e.g. running `git diff` / tests / file exploration) rather than a single judge call. This plugin only emits the spec; the runner (evals-platform) executes it — validate.py never runs it.

harness.description: The agent harness the runner launches. Extensible enum; opencode is the only value today.

sandbox.description: The sandbox the runner provisions for the agent.

network.description: Network policy. Default to the most restrictive the task allows.

allowed_tools.description: Tools the grading agent may use, e.g. ['bash', 'read', 'git'].

task_prompt.description: The grading task the agent performs. Self-contained instructions ending in a single decision, e.g. 'Run `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` and decide whether it correctly implements the requested change.'

verdict_contract.description: How the agent must emit its result so the runner can parse it, e.g. 'print PASS or FAIL on the final line'. v1 verdicts are binary (pass/fail).

score_scale.description: Required for kind=score. The integer range the judge assigns; current convention is min 1, max 5.

rubric_levels.description: Required for kind=score. Maps each level (stringified integer in [min, max]) to a concrete, observable anchor describing output at that level. (For kind=llm_judge the prose `rubric` string is used instead.)

applies_when.description: Natural-language applicability predicate, ALWAYS evaluated by an LLM at runtime (v6). For kind=llm_judge/score it rides inline in the judge prompt; for kind=deterministic the platform runs a separate LLM applicability gate BEFORE the deterministic_check and skips out-of-scope outputs — so the deterministic_check must NOT implement the gate itself. When set, out-of-scope outputs are reported not_applicable at runtime; golden dataset items that fall outside scope are surfaced as not_applicable by the platform runner rather than asserted by the author (v7 removed per-grader self-tests).

_body_source.description: Body-provenance marker for kind=llm_judge / kind=score, the three-state lifecycle (v9; the value was a single 'platform' in v8). DEFERRED — 'platform': the author deferred the verdict body to the platform; the grader carries only the definition (kind, applies_when (llm_judge), rubric_levels+score_scale (score), confidence, rationale) and MUST NOT carry a non-empty judge_prompt/rubric. MATERIALIZED — 'platform-materialized': the platform generated the body and synced it back into the repo (via its GitHub integration); the inline judge_prompt (+rubric for llm_judge) MUST now be PRESENT and is frozen via _meta.locked_fields; _meta.materialized_at and _meta.body_digest are recorded. HUMAN — 'human': a human edited a materialized body in-repo; the body MUST be PRESENT and is treated as the new authoritative body that propagates back upstream on the next platform sync. Absent means the body is plugin-authored inline (the legacy v7 shape). The marker is valid ONLY on llm_judge/score; deterministic/execution/agentic bodies never set it and stay plugin-authored.

judge_prompt.description: Required and non-empty when kind=llm_judge — UNLESS _body_source == 'platform' (v8 deferral), in which case it is deferred to the platform and must be omitted/empty. When _body_source is 'platform-materialized' or 'human' (v9) the platform/human body is PRESENT here and required non-empty.

rubric.description: Required and non-empty when kind=llm_judge — UNLESS _body_source == 'platform' (v8 deferral), in which case it is deferred to the platform and must be omitted/empty. When _body_source is 'platform-materialized' or 'human' (v9) the body is PRESENT here and required non-empty.

confidence.description: Author-assigned confidence in this grader. Behavioral calibration now happens platform-side against golden datasets (real labeled spans), not via per-grader self-tests.

rationale.description: One sentence in user-impact terms for the curation report.

block_on_fail.description: Optional override for the global severity_policy (in pipeline.yaml runtime.severity_policy). When true, a failing grader blocks CI/release regardless of severity. When false, a failing grader is report-only. When absent, the severity_policy default applies.

dataset_refs.description: Optional. Pointers to real inputs this grader should run against — captured trace span IDs or file:line source locations. Populated by orchestrator from Path A trace ingestion or Path B static discovery. Empty/absent means 'no captured dataset'.

pack_ids.description: Set of pack ids this grader's failure mode belongs to (propagated from failure_modes[].pack_ids). Empty / absent = baseline (not pack-contributed). Each entry must resolve to a pipeline.packs[].id; validate.py --bundle enforces.

_meta.description: Provenance + lock metadata used to survive re-runs. Orchestrator writes this on first emission; carries forward across re-syntheses; respects locked_fields on overwrite.

_validation_error.description: Set by the step-6 subagent if validation kept failing after 3 retries. Presence of this key is an explicit accepted-broken signal — validate.py short-circuits all other rules and returns 0 when this key is present, so the file is not re-flagged by later --pipeline cross-check passes. The operator-facing report.md and the failed-validation count in the summary line surface these files.

allOf.comment: trace grades the final turn of a multi-turn session at one call site; routes like single_call.

llm_judge requires an inline judge_prompt+rubric UNLESS the body is deferred to the platform (_body_source == 'platform', v8). v9: 'platform-materialized'/'human' are != 'platform', so this conditional correctly REQUIRES the present body for the materialized/human states.
agentic produces a binary verdict via an agent running in a sandbox; the spec is emitted here, executed by the runner.
Failure-catching kinds carry a failure_mode_id and a taxonomy_node_id.
Score graders always link to a quality dimension and carry an anchored rubric + scale.

A score grader carries an inline judge_prompt UNLESS the body is deferred to the platform (_body_source == 'platform', v8). v9: 'platform-materialized'/'human' are != 'platform', so this conditional correctly REQUIRES the present judge_prompt for the materialized/human states.

dataset_ref.description: One pointer to a real input this grader can be run against.

jsonl_path.description: Path (relative to .tessary/) of a JSONL dataset capturing inputs for this call site, e.g. 'datasets/<call_site_id>.jsonl'.

meta.description: Provenance + lock metadata. Allows re-runs to carry hand-edits forward.

author.description: Identifier of the grader author that produced this file (e.g. 'default', 'evals-prompt').

author_contract_version.description: Contract version the author conformed to. Set by the orchestrator to the version the producing author declared (the bundled default author and evals-prompt both declare 8, the current contract version).

synthesized_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this file was synthesized.

synth_inputs_digest.description: Hex digest (first 16+ chars of SHA-256) over the canonical author input: failure_mode + call_site/chain + product_profile snapshot. Re-runs that produce the same digest may safely overwrite locked_fields=[] graders without diff churn.

locked_fields.description: Fields the orchestrator must preserve verbatim on re-run. Set by humans during curation (or by --lock at the CLI). On overwrite, the orchestrator loads the existing file, retains values for every key in this list, and only refreshes other fields.

human_edited.description: Set to true by curators (or by tooling) to mark the file as touched. Re-runs warn before overwriting human_edited files unless --force. v9: also set automatically when a `platform-materialized` body's recomputed body_digest no longer matches _meta.body_digest (a human edited the materialized body); the grader's _body_source is promoted to 'human' at the same time.

grounding.description: What this grader was authored from. `observed`: the call site's real traces were fetched from the linked project and read before authoring (the default path). `none`: authored under --skip-trace-grounding, from source code alone, so its judge prompt, rubric anchors, and applies_when gate are inferred rather than calibrated against production. Optional and absent on bundles synthesized before trace grounding was mandatory.

materialized_at.description: v9. ISO-8601 timestamp recorded when the platform materialized the verdict body back into this repo file (i.e. when _body_source became 'platform-materialized'). Optional; present only on materialized/human bodies.

body_digest.description: v9. Hex SHA-256 over the CANONICAL verdict body the platform last materialized — the normalized judge_prompt (+ '\n' + rubric for llm_judge), each stripped of trailing whitespace per line and of leading/trailing blank lines. On load, a recomputed digest that differs from this value signals a human edit: the grader is promoted from 'platform-materialized' to 'human' and _meta.human_edited is set true, so the next platform sync propagates the human revision upstream. Optional; present only on materialized/human bodies.
